campgrounds.name: Village Camp Truckee (formerly Coachland RV Park)
campgrounds.pet_notes: Pets welcome; on-site off-leash dog park
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — same loop/section is fine. No stated RV size restrictions, a safe bet for the 35ft motorhome. Private reservation system (not recreation.gov), so inventory isn't subject to the 6-month government booking rush — better odds a month out. Call to hold 3 sites near each other.
campgrounds.notes: Wooded Truckee setting among pines, a short drive (not walk) from Donner Lake itself for swimming/fishing/boating. Cable TV and WiFi included. Top realistic pick given the booking timeline.

campgrounds.name: Plumas Pines Resort
campgrounds.pet_notes: Not confirmed — call to verify pet policy
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — 63 sites gives real flexibility to get 3 in the same area. Private booking system, not government-lottery timed, so a month out is a normal booking window rather than a long shot. $54-150/night depending on site.
campgrounds.notes: Newly in range after expanding the search to 200 miles. Mixed conifer forest right on Lake Almanor (28,000-acre lake, real boating/swimming/fishing), 40ft max fits both big rigs, own marina on site. Furthest of the group at ~125 miles but comfortably inside 200. Skip Canyon Ranch RV Resort nearby — it's marketed as long-term/seasonal stays, not a good fit for a 3-night trip.

campgrounds.name: Zephyr Cove Resort RV Park & Campground
campgrounds.pet_notes: Pet-friendly; dogs welcome on the restaurant deck
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Checked live Recreation.gov availability on 2026-07-07: zero RV-electric sites (of 93) show all three nights of Aug 6-7-8 open at once — only 1-2 scattered single-night gaps. This campground runs on the federal 6-month booking window and August at Tahoe books out within minutes of release, so exact dates are effectively sold out already. Worth a call to ask about phone-held sites or a cancellation waitlist, but don't count on it. Later August (23rd+) had 15-20+ RV sites open per night if your dates have any flexibility.
campgrounds.notes: The best setting of the group — genuine Jeffrey-pine forest a short walk from a Tahoe beach — but treat it as a long shot for Aug 6-9 given real-time availability. Keep as a stretch goal / cancellation watch, not the primary plan.

campgrounds.name: Gold Ranch Casino & RV Resort
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Sites don't need to touch — same section is fine. Large resort with a mix of pull-through and back-in — call and ask for 3 sites near each other. Good odds with a few weeks' notice since it's privately booked.
campgrounds.notes: River canyon setting with scattered cottonwoods and pines rather than dense forest — more valley/foothill than "in the trees." Reliable fallback: closest full-hookup option (~12 miles) with real inventory a month out.

campgrounds.name: River West Resort
campgrounds.adjacency_note: Small, established riverfront park close to downtown — call ahead since availability is tighter than the bigger resorts.
campgrounds.notes: Riverfront park operating since 1927, about a mile from downtown Reno — urban/valley setting, not forested. Lowest-effort option if plans fall through last-minute, but not the tree-and-lake feel you're after.
